LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SANTA BARBARA CHANNELKEEPER'S MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT IS GRANTED FOR THE FOLLOWING REASONS: 1) THE REASONABLE USE AND PUBLIC TRUST DOCTRINES DO NOT REQUIRE THE COURT TO EXAMINE OTHER SPECIFIC COMPETING WATER RIGHTS ON THE RIVER TO RESOLVE THE ACTUAL RELIEF THAT PLAINTIFF IS REQUESTING; 2) THE COURT FINDS THAT THE CITY OF BUENAVENTURA'S CROSS-COMPLAINT DOES NOT ARISE FROM THE SAME TRANSACTION OR OCCURENCE AS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM BECAUSE THE ONLY TRANSACTION AT ISSUE IN PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS WHETHER OR NOT THE CITY OF BUENAVENTURA'S WATER USE IS UNREASONABLE 3) THE COURT FINDS THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM DOES NOT IMPLICATE A PROPERTY RIGHT. ONLY THE CITY'S UNREASONABLE USE OF THE RIVER IS AT ISSUE AND FINALLY, THE CITY OF BUENAVENTURA'S CROSS-COMPLAINT JOINING OF NUMEROUS OTHER PARTIES THAT THE CITY WISHES TO JOIN DOES NOT PREVENT A MULTIPLICITY OF SUITS OR SERVE THE PURPOSE OF JUDICIAL ECONOMY. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER AND TO COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: SUZANNE BOLANOS; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER : MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489 =302.SB

Motion To Strike First Amended Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Aug-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CAROLINE A. KOCH & ATTY. DANIEL G. COOPER OF THE LAWYERS FOR CLEAN WATER, TEL. NO. 1-415-440-6520 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. GENE TANAKA & ATTY. SHAWN D. HAGERTY OF THE BEST BEST & KRIEGER, TEL. NO. 1-925-977-3301 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT CITY OF SAN BUENAVENTURA;
ATTY. MATTHEW BULLOCK, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-415-703-1678 APPEARING FOR THE STATE WATER BOARD.

RULING - A R G U E D; PETITIONER SANTA BARBARA CHANNELKEEPER'S Motion To Strike First Amended Cross-Complaint is granted for the following reasons: 1) the reasonable use and public trust doctrines do not require the court to examine other specific competing water rights on the river to resolve the actual relief that Plaintiff is requesting; 2) The court finds that the City of Buenaventura's cross-complaint does not arise from the same transaction or occurence as Plaintiff's claim because the only transaction at issue in plaintiff's complaint is whether or not the City of Buenaventura's water use is unreasonable 3) The court finds that plaintiff's claim does not implicate a property right. Only the city's unreasonable use of the river is at issue and finally, the City of Buenaventura's cross-complaint joining of numerous other parties that the city wishes to join does not prevent a multiplicity of suits or serve the purpose of judicial economy. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302.sb

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT CITY OF BUENAVENTURA'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE - The City of San Buenaventura's demurrer to and motion to strike the first count is overruled and denied with twenty days to answer. The City has not shown that there is a misjoinder or that any other party is necessary or indispensable to the adjudication of the first count. The complaint does not clearly disclose that it is time-barred. The applicable limitations statute is Code of Civil Procedure section 338, not Water Code section 13330. The notice requirements of the federal Clean Water Act and the federal Endangered Species Act are inapplicable to this case. Order signed in open court. ATTY. GENER TANAKA OF THE BEST BEST & KRIEGER LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. (925) 977-3301 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT CITY OF BUENAVENTURA.

RULING - A R G U E D; RESPONDENT CITY OF BUENAVENTURA'S Motion To Strike Complaint and Petition for Writ of Mandate - The City of San Buenaventura's demurrer to and motion to strike the first count is overruled and denied with twenty days to answer. The City has not shown that there is a misjoinder or that any other party is necessary or indispensable to the adjudication of the first count. The complaint does not clearly disclose that it is time-barred. The applicable limitations statute is Code of Civil Procedure section 338, not Water Code section 13330. The notice requirements of the federal Clean Water Act and the federal Endangered Species Act are inapplicable to this case. Order signed in open court.
